strategy: Loon Knight polymorphs its Luna-Tick minions into duck forms (Old Quack, and with 3+ wizards also Quentin Quail and Drake Lockley), each punishing a different action; the fight is about avoiding their triggers.
Watch your buffs: Old Quack Enfeebles off any universal helpful charm, multi-blade, or off-school blade, and Aegis will not save the blade; use plain single-school blades or bank buffs.
Around Quentin Quail, every hanging effect you cast (charm, ward, jinx) draws a counter-spell such as Weakness, Balanceblade, Hex, or shields; keep setup minimal.
Around Drake Lockley, any damage or pierce charm, trap, aura, or Global earns an 800 Storm Duck Savage on the caster; avoid those categories.
Every third Round Loon Knight hits a random wizard with Shatterhorn (strips shields), and when minions run out it Polymorphs itself into the duck forms, keeping their cheats.
Set up as little as possible, kill the duck minions to force Loon Knight to polymorph, then burst it down.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 it turns its minions into duck forms; the number depends on party size, and each form has its own punish cheat.
Every third Round it Shatterhorns a random wizard, stripping shields; keep a heal ready rather than relying on shields.
When no minions remain it Polymorphs itself into a duck form, keeping all its cheats plus that form's; killing minions does not remove the danger.
It can Polymorph again into a second form, stacking both forms' cheats; expect layered punishes late.
Old Quack Enfeebles off any universal helpful charm or multi-blade you cast; use single-school blades, and Aegis will not protect them.
A multi-target blade makes it Enfeeble your whole team's blades; avoid Bladestorm-type buffs.
Casting a blade outside your own school also triggers the Enfeeble.
Blades that are secondary effects, like Savage Paw's, do not trigger the Enfeeble.
Aegis does not stop this blade removal, so do not rely on it here.
Quentin Quail answers each hanging effect you cast with a matching counter-spell; keep your charms and wards to a minimum.
Even secondary hanging effects, like a spell's built-in blade, trigger Quentin's counters.
A single helpful charm earns a Weakness on the receiver.
A team helpful charm earns a Mass Weakness.
A single harmful charm on an enemy gives it a Balanceblade.
Starfall also triggers the harmful-charm counter.
A harmful charm on all enemies gives them a +25% Bladestorm.
A single helpful ward earns a Hex.
A team helpful ward earns a Mass Hex.
A single harmful ward on an enemy gives it a Tower Shield.
Attenuate also triggers the harmful-ward counter.
A harmful ward on all enemies gives them Legion Shield.
Drake Lockley hits the caster with an 800 Storm Duck Savage for any damage or pierce charm, trap, aura, or Global; avoid those categories.
Multi-target versions land the Duck Savage on the caster too.
Secondary effects also trigger Drake's Duck Savage, so check side effects before casting.
